pullRequest.title: FIX: detect and correct prematurely incremented stint counter in tyre…
pullRequest.body:
In two recent sessions, tyre data was incorrect for some or all drivers. This is caused by incorrect source data. The PR implements additional logic to detect these errors in the source data and fix them. This is done by cross-referencing the tyre data stint counter with the ``PitInTimes`` from the lap data. The main premise is that the stint counter can only increase by a maximum of one between two subsequent times when a car enters the pit.

@Casper-Guo, quoting your comment in #742
> ```
>     Driver                   Time      Compound  StartLaps    New  Stint
> 18      87 0 days 00:40:13.871000  INTERMEDIATE        0.0   True      0
> 36      87 0 days 01:11:54.121000  INTERMEDIATE        0.0   True      1
> 72      87 0 days 01:13:44.098000          None        NaN   None      0
> 73      87 0 days 01:13:44.098000          None        NaN   None      1
> 192     87 0 days 01:22:29.149000          None        NaN   None      0
> 193     87 0 days 01:22:29.149000          None        NaN   None      1
> 194     87 0 days 01:22:29.226000          None        NaN   None      1
> 247     87 0 days 01:34:49.193000        MEDIUM        5.0  False      1
> 271     87 0 days 01:36:24.226000          None        NaN   None      1
> 294     87 0 days 01:38:14.234000          None        NaN   None      0
> 295     87 0 days 01:38:14.234000          None        NaN   None      1
> 317     87 0 days 01:40:34.252000          None        NaN   None      1
> 339     87 0 days 01:42:54.274000          None        NaN   None      1
> 354     87 0 days 01:45:09.262000          None        NaN   None      1
> 373     87 0 days 01:47:24.285000          None        NaN   None      1
> ```
> 
> We are backfilling the medium compound all the way back to the first timestamp because for whatever reason the stint column is not incremented.

Your conclusion was not fully correct. In fact, the stint counter is prematurely incremented in the second row without there being a tyre change. From then on, the two sets of intermediate tyres (which are the same set really) coexist throughout the first actual stint in the data until the driver changes to medium tyres.  The medium compound is then incorrectly backfilled because the stint counter was wrong in the preceding stint.

Here are two (not very nicely visualized) tyre strategy comparisons for the two affected sessions. Left side before this change, right side is after.

![Picture2](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/47b659f0-2ab8-4c3b-8c03-aa721dd2a3f5)

![Picture1](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/504e26a7-5460-419f-bd47-0319d20801ba)

(Alonso's crash outlap in Miami is missing, therefore, the medium tyre is never shown for him in the graphic. But that's a different problem.)

Closes #742, closes #715

pullRequest.title: Proper initiation of NaT as timedelta type
pullRequest.body:
Addresses a followup report from #674 

Essentially the same bug and fix as in #682 and #676. Just need to bypass pandas inferring NaT as a datetime.

Fixes #728

pullRequest.title: ENH: expose number of laps in session results for race-like sessions
MNT/FIX: Refactor exceptions into submodule and introduce critical exceptions
pullRequest.body:
- All existing exceptions are refactored into a new ``fastf1.exceptions`` module. This simplifies access and reduces the risk for circular imports.

- A new ``FastF1CriticalError`` is introduced. It serves as a base class for all exceptions that are raised by FastF1during data loading and need to break through the ``soft_exceptions`` decorator. These exceptions are always raised, and data loading is therefore terminated.

- The ``RateLimitExceededError`` now derives from the ``FastF1CriticalError`` to ensure that rate limit errors are always raised.

closes #748

provides some work towards the goals in #541

pullRequest.title: FIX: position calc does not ignore crashed drivers
ENH: fill in speed trap values missing in src data
pullRequest.body:
Fill in missing speed trap values using a conditional forward fill.

Closes #775

pullRequest.title: FIX: trim support race drivers from driver list
pullRequest.body:
In some sessions, the driver info endpoint accidentally includes driver info from a later support race (F2, F3, ...). Trim the data accordingly so that these drivers are not included in the F1 data.

closes #794

pullRequest.title: FIX: correct timestamps of bunched up tyre data
pullRequest.body:
### PR summary

Detects when tyre data was delayed at the start of a session and all bunched up messages then have the same timestamp. In those cases, the timestamps are corrected so that they line up with their corresponding stints. This fixes issues with missing tyre data in some sessions.

Closes #863

##### ToDo

- ~~include proposed refactoring of tyre data parser from #872~~ implemented separately in #903
- test against other sessions to ensure this breaks nothing
- assumptions made may only hold for race-like sessions:  test and evaluate

#### AI Disclosure
No AI tools used

pullRequest.title: FIX: invalid first lap added for DNS drivers that have tyre data
pullRequest.body:
### PR summary
<!-- Please take some time to make it easier for us maintainers to understand
  and review your PR. Describe the pull request in your own words and link to 
  any relevant issues and PRs.

  You should also read [the contribution guide](https://docs.fastf1.dev/contributing/index.html)
  before creating a PR.
-->

This fixes #876 where an invalid first lap was added for drivers who didn't start the race (DNS). This happened because tyre data for a planned first stint was available, causing FastF1 to handle this as if the driver crashed on the first lap of the race.
Now, result data will be used (if available), to differentiate this new special case.

Using result data while loading lap timing data is not desirable, due to the mixed data sources and because the data will not be available immediately after the race. Right now, no different solution for this problem seems to exists, though.

closes #876
closes #881

#### AI Disclosure

  the tool(s) used, how they were used, and specify what code or text is AI generated.

pullRequest.title: Update ruff pre-commit hook and fail CI on warnings
pullRequest.body:
### PR summary

Fixes #878

Updated the ruff pre-commit hook from v0.12.7 to v0.15.8 and switched from the legacy `ruff` hook id to `ruff-check`.

For the CI action, ruff doesn't have a native flag to turn warnings into errors (astral-sh/ruff#8157), so the step now pipes output through grep and fails the job if any warnings show up.

Removed 2 stale noqa directives that ruff no longer needs:
- `# noqa: F821` in `core.py` (variable isn't undefined anymore)
- `# noqa: F401` in `plotting/__init__.py` (ruff now recognizes `__all__` re-exports)

Also added `RUF100` to the lint config so unused noqa directives get caught going forward.

#### AI Disclosure

AI tools were used to help locate the stale noqa directives and draft the CI grep step. All changes were reviewed and tested locally by me.

pullRequest.title: DOC: Add Ai policy
pullRequest.body:
Add an AI policy to the contribution guide in the documentation. Also add a pull request template that requires the disclosure of AI usage and links to the AI policy. This is similar to the approach that many large open source projects have already taken.

closes #879
